hypothesis_en: Mr President, I greet with affection all the Members and especially the other candidates. I belong to the generation of Spaniards who, with Europe on the horizon, played a major role in the transition to democracy and very young, assumed important political responsibilities. I have also been a member of the Convention and for ten consecutive years I have represented my country in various Council formations. I can say that I am well aware of the functioning of the Community institutions, but Europe is for me more than a political experience, a vital project. I am European as much as I am Spanish and as much as I am Catalan, but I am not from the old or the new Europe. I am simply a European who rejects denominations intended to perpetuate our division. We have all been new to the Union at some point, and from Vilnius to Lisbon or from Edinburgh to Athens we are all the same Europe. I will ensure that it functions properly in a more complex linguistic context. We need to eliminate any shadow area that discredits this Parliament. We need the Statute so that Members from all countries can exercise their function under the same conditions of transparency, dignity and effectiveness. Mr President, in the coming years this Parliament will be at the crossroads of history. We have come to it by the path of success. Yesterday's secular enemies are today the most cooperative neighbours in the world. But today the European project suffers from a triple problem of dimension, effectiveness and legitimacy to which the Constitution, to whose drafting I have contributed, contributes. Without the Constitution we would be, for a long time, just a big market, and if Europe abandoned its political ambition, the world would suffer the destructive confrontation of materialism on the one hand and fundamentalism on the other. That will be a great moment for this Parliament to talk about Europe to the Europeans, to convince them that ours is a living project, with a soul, although it is inevitably complex, that affects the problems that most concern them, employment, security, terrorism and migration, problems that would have no solution. We must project this institution onto the citizens we represent by making our message understandable. In a Europe with 90 unemployed and 50 million poor, we must make clear the importance of Community policies, the Lisbon strategy, the Gothenburg environmental dimension, on the cohesion and competitiveness that we need. We all want a close and close President of Parliament, but his role is not that of a head of government who implements a partisan political programme. And I end with a message of optimism: Europe is a long-term project that is making its way, the crises ahead will not be more serious than those we have already overcome. I would like to thank the President-in-Office of the Council and the President of the European Parliament.
reference_en: Fontelles has the floor first of all Mr President let me warmly greet all Members of the European Parliament and the other candidates in particular I'm from that generation of Spaniards who helped Spain ca= carry out its transition to democracy we were very young back then but we already took up very important political responsibilities I was a shop steward and a trade union activist a member of the national Parliament for eighteen years I was president of the foreign affairs committee for five years and I spent twelve years as a minister or secretary of state and was a candidate for the presidency of the government I was also a member of the Convention (1.13) and for ten years represented (1.51) Spain in various Council formations (1.24) there I lost certain battles which later were won in the European Parliament because of a s= vision that was closer to that of our citizens I think I can say that I do understand well how the relations between the three institutions work but Europe is more than a political experience for me it's a vital project I'm a European just as I'm a Spaniard and as I'm a Catalan but I'm not from the old or from the new Europe I'm just an ordinary European one who rejects labels which will perpetuate our division all of us have been at some time new members of the European Union and from Vilnius to Lisbon or from Edinburgh to Athens we're all part of the same Europe reunification ended the grim heritage of Hitler and Stalin but there's still a lot to do to make reunification real it'll test our solidarity it'll require respect for differing viewpoints as President of the European Parliament I will make sure that it works appropriately in a more complicated linguistic context friends we need to eliminate any kind of twilight zone which overshadows this Parliament we need a statute so that members from all countries can carry out their jobs in the same issues or areas of transparency dignity and effectiveness so I will begin as I have to do with our relations with Council friends in the next few years Parliament will be at the crossroads of history we've arrived there having successfully travelled a road where yesterday's enemies are the most cooperative neighbours in the world today but the European project today has got a tr= try= triple problem of size legitimacy and effectiveness and the Constitution whose (1.4) drafting I help participate in does I think provide some good responses even though I'm not totally satisfied with it without the Constitution we will simply remain a large market and if Europe abandons its political ambitions the world will suffer from the destructive clash between materialism on one side and fundamentalism on the other (1.35) so (1.15) I call on all Members of this House to take up the great task of c= ratifying the Constitution this will be a tremendous moment for this Parliament speaking about a Europe of Europeans trying to convince Europeans that this is a real project one with a soul even though it is of course a complicated issue but it's one that affects and deals with problems which they're most concerned about employment security terrorism migration problems which won't have any solution outside Europe we need to build this institution (1.59) basing it upon the citizenship which we represent speaking a language which people can understand (1.09) in a Europe with nine percent unemployment and fifty million poor people we need to make sure that people realise the importance of political policies the Lisbon strategy the environmental dimension of Gothenburg and the impact that they have on competitiveness and cohesion which aren't mutually exclusive but complementary as far as we're concerned we want a president of Parliament who's accessible and effective but he's not obviously a head of government he doesn't apply a party political programme the President needs to enjoy broad support and he needs to play the role of a strategic driving force for the institution I'm very much aware of this if I'm elected I'll represent the diversity of this House and I will defend its role before the other community institutions let me end on an optimistic note Europe is a long-term project (1.03) it traces out its course as it travels along the crises which await us won't be more serious than those which we've already overcome in the past we can and we must inspire the peoples of Europe calling upon them to write a new page in our history of peace and for that reason and to help achieve that I ask for you to vote for me as president of the European Parliament thank you very much
source_es: señor Presidente saludo con afecto a todos los diputados y diputadas y especialmente a los demás candidatos pertenezco a la generación de españoles que con Europa en el horizonte protagonizó la transición a la democracia y muy jóvenes asumimos importantes responsabilidades políticas desde concejal y delegado sindical parlamentario durante dieciocho años cinco presidiendo la Comisión de Asuntos Europeos y doce años como ministro o secretario de Estado hasta candidato a la presidencia del Gobierno (1.07) también he sido miembro de la Convención y durante diez años seguidos he representado (1.07) a mi país en distintas formaciones del Consejo allí perdí batallas que luego se ganaron en el Parlamento desde una visión más próxima a los ciudadanos puedo decir que conozco bien el funcionamiento de las instituciones comunitarias pero Europa es para mí más que una experiencia política un proyecto vital soy europeo tanto como soy español y tanto com també sóc català pero no soy de la vieja ni de la nueva Europa soy simplemente un europeo que rechaza denominaciones destinadas a perpetuar nuestra división (1.25) todos hemos sido alguna vez nuevos en la Unión y desde Vilnius hasta Lisboa o desde Edimburgo hasta Atenas todos somos la misma Europa (1.18) la reunificación acabó con la triste herencia de Hitler y de Stalin pero nos queda mucho para hacerla efectiva pondrá a prueba nuestra solidaridad y exigirá respeto para opiniones más diversas como Presidente del Parlamento Europeo aseguraré su adecuado funcionamiento en un contexto lingüístico más complejo amigos tenemos que eliminar cualquier zona de sombra que desprestigie a este Parlamento (1.01) necesitamos el Estatuto para que los diputados de todos los países puedan ejercer su función en las mismas condiciones de transparencia dignidad y eficacia así empezaré con exigencia nuestra relación con el Consejo (1.02) amigos en los próximos años este Parlamento estará en la encrucijada de la historia a ella hemos llegado por el camino de un éxito los enemigos seculares de ayer son hoy los vecinos más cooperativos del mundo pero hoy el proyecto europeo sufre un triple problema de dimensión eficacia y legitimidad al que la Constitución a cuya redacción he contribuido aporta la mejor respuesta que tenemos sin la Constitución seríamos y por mucho tiempo sólo un gran mercado y si Europa abandonara su ambición política el mundo sufriría el destructor enfrentamiento del materialismo por una parte y del fundamentalismo por la otra por ello convocaré a todos los parlamentarios a la gran tarea de la ratificación constitucional (1.15) ese será un gran momento para este Parlamento para hablar de Europa a los europeos para convencerles de que el nuestro es un proyecto vivo con alma aunque sea inevitablemente complejo que afecta a los problemas que más les preocupan el empleo sí el empleo la seguridad el terrorismo y las migraciones problemas que no tendrían solución fuera de Europa (1.07) hemos de proyectar esta institución sobre la ciudadanía que representamos haciendo inteligible nuestro mensaje en una Europa con el noventa por ciento del paro y cincuenta millones de pobres hemos de hacer entender la importancia que las políticas comunitarias la estrategia de Lisboa la dimensión ambiental de Gothenburg tienen sobre la cohesión y la competitividad que para nosotros no son antagónicas sino complementarias amigos todos queremos un presidente del Parlamento cercano y afín pero su papel no es el de un jefe de gobierno que aplica un programa político partidario el Presidente tiene que tener un apoyo sólido y jugar un papel de impulso estratégico para la institución que representa soy muy consciente de ello si soy elegido representaré la diversidad del Parlamento y con firmeza su papel ante las demás instituciones comunitarias y acabo con un mensaje de optimismo Europa es un proyecto que se inscribe en el largo plazo y que hace su camino al andar las crisis que nos esperan no serán más graves que las que ya hemos superado debemos y podemos movilizar a los pueblos de Europa para escribir una nueva página de nuestra historia en paz y por ello y para ello les pido su voto a mi candidatura a la presidencia del Parlamento Europeo muchas gracias
